editorial_thesis: Today’s practical signal is operational control: make agent authority explicit, patch exposed runtimes, and measure adoption by completed work rather than activity.

topic_groups.title: Agent Sandbox + Tool Controls
topic_groups.selection_rationale: This is a fresh control-surface lane, selected after several days centered on evaluation and adoption: new releases make credential injection, harness substitution, and MCP exposure explicit configuration choices rather than hidden framework behavior.

sources.title: Agents SDK reduces MCP schema conversion and adds exposure controls
sources.why_selected: Automatic tool discovery is convenient but expands an agent’s effective authority. An explicit opt-in creates a review point.
sources.usefulness: Prototype one MCP workflow with `includeMcpTools = false`; add only the two tools required for its acceptance test.

sources.title: Safely inject credentials in HTTP headers with Vercel Sandbox
sources.why_selected: It separates agent runtime access from credential possession and supports a setup phase followed by deny-all execution.
sources.usefulness: Turn an existing sandbox policy into a two-phase policy: authenticated setup, then deny-all execution.

sources.title: Program Claude Code, Codex, Pi and other agent harnesses with AI SDK
sources.why_selected: Treating the harness as a replaceable implementation lets a workflow retain its policy, tools, skills, and acceptance checks while evaluation compares runners.
sources.usefulness: Pick one repetitive repair task and run it through two harnesses under identical limits.

sources.title: Gemini Enterprise supports unauthenticated Custom MCP Server data stores (Preview)
sources.why_selected: This is a sharp reminder that “easy connector setup” can turn an unauthenticated endpoint into part of a business answer path.
sources.usefulness: Add “connector authentication and audience” to the agent integration review checklist.

topic_groups.title: Web Runtime Patch + Access Hygiene
topic_groups.selection_rationale: This lane rotates away from repeated model news toward immediate web-stack maintenance. The sources are primary notices with concrete versions, dates, and least-privilege changes that can become a short client operations checklist.

sources.title: Next.js July 2026 Security Release
sources.why_selected: The release turns a broad framework risk into concrete upgrade targets, including issues relevant to App Router, Server Actions, and server-side fetch.
sources.usefulness: Open a patch ticket today with owner, deployed version, target version, test route, and rollout window.

sources.title: Upcoming GHES change impacting uploading support bundles
sources.why_selected: Supportability is an operational dependency: an appliance that cannot upload diagnostic bundles is harder to recover precisely when an incident needs vendor help.
sources.usefulness: Check GHES inventory and schedule required patching before the August 18 enforcement date.

sources.title: R2 Data Catalog now supports read-only API tokens
sources.why_selected: Analytics and retrieval clients no longer need a write-capable credential merely to list tables or query data.
sources.usefulness: Audit catalog consumers and replace every query-only credential with storage-and-catalog read-only scope.

sources.title: View total SQLite storage for Durable Object namespaces
sources.why_selected: Stateful agent and collaboration workflows can fail quietly through retention and growth; an aggregate time series makes cleanup assumptions testable.
sources.usefulness: Review the largest stateful namespace, document retention behavior, and set a weekly growth check.

topic_groups.title: Agent Adoption Measurement
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Recent dossiers covered agent evaluation and cost concepts, but not the new organizational surface for measuring developer adoption. This lane earns inclusion because a fresh GitHub dashboard provides a concrete starting point for separating activity from delivered impact.

sources.title: New Copilot usage metrics impact dashboard
sources.why_selected: It provides a native administrative surface for adoption conversations instead of relying on anecdotal prompts or license counts.
sources.usefulness: Define a four-week pilot baseline before enabling a new agent capability for a selected team.

sources.title: Access and share AI Gateway leaderboard data
sources.why_selected: External production-routing data can seed model-shortlisting hypotheses, but it should not replace a workload-specific evaluation.
sources.usefulness: Write a one-page model scorecard before changing a production default.

sources.title: Vercel AI Gateway Production Index — July 2026
sources.why_selected: The split is a useful warning against treating token volume, capability, and operating economics as the same metric.
sources.usefulness: Add outcome-per-dollar and fallback rate to the next agent workflow review.

sources.title: OpenAI: A scorecard for the AI age
sources.why_selected: It reinforces a customer-facing measurement frame: value comes from finished, dependable work rather than model theater.
sources.usefulness: Make “completed work with evidence” the primary success criterion for the next client pilot.
